docs.title: Conclusion
docs.text: The diagram suite is highly consistent and conforms to both technical conventions (such as DBML schemas) and business specifications. Immediate resolution should focus on deduplicating copy-pasted diagrams and fixing minor syntax warnings.

docs.title: Diagram Gap Analysis Report
docs.text: Field Value Document ID EDUSYNC-GAP-DIAG-001 Version 1.0.0 Status Draft Author Pushpraj Jaiswal Created 2026-07-02 Last Updated 2026-07-02 Confidentiality Internal

docs.title: Revision History
docs.text: Version Date Author Status Changes 1.0.0 2026-07-02 Pushpraj Jaiswal Draft Initial diagram gap analysis report

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
1. Executive Summary
  2. Purpose
  3. Objectives
  4. Scope
  5. Out of Scope
  6. Definitions
  7. Gap Analysis Recommendations
  8. Conclusion

docs.title: Executive Summary
docs.text: An analysis was conducted to identify documentation files that do not contain visual elements or diagrams. Out of 24 core documentation files analyzed, 20 contain diagrams and 4 do not. This report details recommendations to close these visualization gaps.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: To ensure maximum clarity for developers and stakeholders by identifying files lacking visual diagrams and proposing relevant toolsets (Mermaid, Figma, draw.io, Structurizr, dbdiagram) to address them.

docs.title: Objectives
docs.text:
• Detect all diagram-free files in the core documentation path.
  • Select appropriate visualization tools for each missing context.
  • Define implementation priority based on development impact.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text: Applies to core product, business, academic, UI/UX, database, API, and engineering documentation. Excludes asset templates and specific specs.

docs.title: Out of Scope
docs.text: Generating the actual diagrams for these files is out of scope for this gap analysis report.

docs.title: Definitions
docs.text: Tool Primary Diagram Benefit Mermaid Dynamic text-to-diagram flowcharts, sequences, state diagrams inside markdown draw.io Detailed enterprise workflow, business process, and custom complex shape layout diagrams dbdiagram Interactive database schema modeling and relationship diagram generation Structurizr Software architecture modeling using the C4 model DSL Figma High-fidelity user interface layouts, interactive prototypes, design system guidelines

docs.title: Gap Analysis Recommendations
docs.text: Document Recommended Diagram Tool Reason Priority docs/03-Product-Requirements/user-stories.md Mermaid Benefit from a user journey map or story map using Mermaid timeline/gitgraph diagrams to show sequencing. Low docs/03-Product-Requirements/user-personas.md Figma Benefit from user persona layout cards and target segment visual profile boards designed in Figma. Medium docs/03-Product-Requirements/use-cases.md Mermaid Benefit from Mermaid flowchart/state diagrams to model complex use case flows, exceptions, and actor interactions. High docs/05-UI-UX/Figma-Planning.md Figma Benefit from direct Figma frame embed codes and component hierarchy maps representing the screen layouts. High

docs.title: Conclusion
docs.text: Adding diagrams to use cases and UI/UX planning documents should be prioritized first to provide clear implementation targets for engineering and design teams.

docs.title: Diagrams
docs.text:
This folder is the central repository for all EduSync documentation diagrams.

It contains source files, exported assets, and supporting documentation for architecture, business, database, API, security, deployment, and wireframe-related diagrams.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Use this directory to organize diagrams according to their domain and intended audience. Diagrams should remain version controlled, maintainable, and linked from the relevant documentation pages.

docs.title: Structure
docs.text:
• architecture/: system and component architecture diagrams
  • database/: entity relationship and data model diagrams
  • business/: business workflows, process flows, and domain interaction diagrams
  • api/: API interaction and integration diagrams
  • security/: authentication, authorization, and security architecture diagrams
  • deployment/: runtime, infrastructure, and deployment topology diagrams
  • wireframes/: UI flows, wireframes, and experience diagrams
  • exports/: exported image assets for documentation and presentations

docs.title: API Diagrams
docs.text:
This folder contains API and integration diagrams for EduSync.

Use this location for:

  • API interaction flows
  • service-to-service communications
  • gateway and integration patterns
  • request and response sequences
  • external integration topology

These diagrams should support API design, system integration, and development planning.

docs.title: Architecture Diagrams
docs.text:
This folder contains architecture-focused diagrams for EduSync.

Use this location for:

  • system context diagrams
  • container diagrams
  • component diagrams
  • high-level solution architecture views
  • C4 model diagrams

These diagrams should describe structure, responsibilities, and major technology boundaries.

docs.title: Business Diagrams
docs.text:
This folder contains business-oriented diagrams for EduSync.

Use this location for:

  • business process flows
  • workflow diagrams
  • operational process maps
  • role-based process views
  • policy and governance flows

These diagrams should communicate how the platform supports school operations and business workflows.

docs.title: Database Diagrams
docs.text:
This folder contains database and data model diagrams for EduSync.

Use this location for:

  • entity relationship diagrams
  • data model views
  • relational schema diagrams
  • domain model relationships
  • tenant-aware data structure views

These diagrams should support database design, implementation planning, and documentation clarity.

docs.title: Deployment Diagrams
docs.text:
This folder contains deployment and infrastructure diagrams for EduSync.

Use this location for:

  • deployment topology diagrams
  • cloud and container architecture views
  • environment and runtime diagrams
  • infrastructure and networking views
  • observability and operations diagrams

These diagrams should support DevOps planning, delivery operations, and environment documentation.

docs.title: Exported Diagram Assets
docs.text:
This folder contains exported diagram artifacts for EduSync documentation.

Use this location for:

  • PNG exports
  • SVG exports
  • PDF exports
  • presentation-ready versions of diagrams
  • generated assets referenced from documentation pages

Exported files should be kept in sync with their editable source diagrams.

docs.title: Security Diagrams
docs.text:
This folder contains security-focused diagrams for EduSync.

Use this location for:

  • authentication and authorization flows
  • identity and access control views
  • tenant isolation diagrams
  • audit and compliance visuals
  • security boundary diagrams

These diagrams should emphasize trust, access control, and data protection across the platform.

docs.title: Draw.io Diagram Specifications
docs.text: This folder contains draw.io design specifications for diagrams that are too detailed, layered, or frequently evolving to remain maintainable in Mermaid.

docs.title: Included Specifications
docs.text:
1. vision-opportunity-map.drawio-spec.md Reason: The strategic opportunity map has multiple parallel branches and would benefit from a manually arranged, presentation-friendly canvas.

  2. business-context.drawio-spec.md Reason: The business context diagram spans market, school, solution, capability, and value layers and is easier to review in a structured draw.io layout.

  3. product-architecture-context.drawio-spec.md Reason: The product architecture context includes many domain modules and platform services that need precise grouping and frequent revision.

  4. software-system-context.drawio-spec.md Reason: The software system context is a multi-layer technical topology with external integrations and operational concerns that are better expressed in draw.io.

  5. proposed-future-workflow.drawio-spec.md Reason: The proposed future workflow connects numerous setup, operations, dashboarding, communication, and audit systems, leading to a spiderweb of overlapping connections in Mermaid.

  6. module-dependency-map.drawio-spec.md Reason: Represents highly interconnected domain modules with deep compilation and execution dependency chains that are extremely difficult to layout clearly in Mermaid code.

  7. vision-model.drawio-spec.md Reason: A massive 26-node tree model representing high-level product vision and functional domains that requires professional vertical card stacking.

  8. swot-analysis.drawio-spec.md Reason: A matrix-style 2x2 grid representing internal and external factors that cannot be natively formatted in a standard Mermaid flowchart.

  9. monitoring-flow.drawio-spec.md Reason: A telemetry pipeline linking application components, databases, and queues through multiple telemetry lines (logs, metrics, traces) into a central observability engine and alerts.

docs.title: draw.io Design Specification — Business Context Diagram
docs.text:
• File name: business-context.drawio
  • Source context: docs/02-Business-Requirements/business-requirements.md

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Show the business relationship between the education market, schools, operational pain, the EduSync solution, and the value created for school stakeholders and the SaaS business.

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• Market and demand
  • Indian school education market
  • Private schools and coaching institutes
  • Problem layer
  • Operational fragmentation
  • Unified digital operations platform
  • EduSync solution layer
  • Administration
  • Academic operations
  • Fees and payments
  • Parent and staff communication
  • Dashboards and AI insights
  • Outcome layer
  • Reduced manual work
  • Improved academic tracking
  • Better collection visibility
  • Higher parent trust
  • Data-driven leadership

docs.title: Layout
docs.text:
• Use a left-to-right flow with a central solution block.
  • Position market and schools on the left.
  • Place problem and solution in the middle.
  • Place capability modules and outcomes on the right.
  • Use rounded containers for each domain cluster.

docs.title: Connections
docs.text:
• Market -> schools -> operational fragmentation -> unified digital operations platform.
  • Unified digital operations platform -> each capability domain.
  • Each capability domain -> one business outcome.
  • Keep the visual path simple and avoid overconnecting every node to every other node.

docs.title: Color Recommendations
docs.text:
• Market: lavender (#7C3AED)
  • Schools and demand: soft blue (#3B82F6)
  • Pain: coral (#EF4444)
  • Solution: navy (#1D4ED8)
  • Capability domains: indigo (#4338CA)
  • Outcomes: emerald (#10B981)
  • Background: very light gray (#FAFAFA)

docs.title: Swimlanes
docs.text:
• Recommended swimlanes: Market, Problem, Solution, Value
  • A four-lane structure will make the directional flow easier to follow.

docs.title: draw.io Design Specification — Module Dependency Map
docs.text:
• File name: module-dependency-map.drawio
  • Source context: docs/03-Product-Requirements/product-requirements.md

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Document the module-level dependencies within the EduSync modular platform. This architecture map defines the compilation and execution dependencies between different modules (such as Authentication, School Core, Academics, Finance, and Reporting) to ensure modular isolation and clean boundaries.

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• System Core & Identity
  • Subscription
  • Authentication
  • School
  • Core Entities
  • Student
  • Teacher
  • Guardian
  • Academic Operations
  • Attendance
  • Homework
  • Examination
  • Financial Workflows
  • Fees
  • Payments
  • Reporting & Dashboards
  • Reports
  • Dashboard
  • Cross-Cutting Utilities
  • Notification

docs.title: Layout
docs.text:
• Use a top-to-bottom layered architecture:
  • Layer 1: Entry & Access Controls (Subscription, Authentication)
  • Layer 2: Core Institution Context (School, Student, Teacher, Guardian)
  • Layer 3: Functional Domain Modules (Attendance, Homework, Examination, Fees, Payments)
  • Layer 4: Aggregation & Consumption (Reports, Dashboard, Notification)
  • Use rectangular boxes with subtle rounded corners for modules.
  • Use explicit grouping borders or backgrounds to mark functional blocks (e.g., Core, Academics, Finance).
  • Ensure connectors flow downwards or horizontally to indicate execution paths clearly without overlapping.

docs.title: Connections
docs.text:
• Connect Subscription to both Authentication and Notification.
  • Connect Authentication to School.
  • Connect School to Student, Teacher, Attendance, Homework, and Examination.
  • Connect Student to Guardian, Attendance, Homework, Examination, and Fees.
  • Connect Teacher to Attendance, Homework, and Examination.
  • Connect Guardian to Fees.
  • Connect Fees to Payments.
  • Connect Attendance, Homework, Examination, Fees, and Payments directly to Reports.
  • Connect Reports to Dashboard.
  • Connect Notification to Dashboard.
  • Use solid arrows for hard compile-time dependencies, and dashed arrows for soft/event-driven dependencies (like Notification and Subscription).

docs.title: Color Recommendations
docs.text:
• System Access & Subscription: Coral (#FEE2E2) with Red border (#EF4444)
  • Core Context (School/People): Sky Blue (#E0F2FE) with Blue border (#0284C7)
  • Academics: Soft Emerald (#D1FAE5) with Green border (#10B981)
  • Finance: Pale Yellow (#FEF3C7) with Gold border (#D97706)
  • Reporting & Dashboards: Lavender (#F3E8FF) with Violet border (#8B5CF6)
  • Cross-Cutting (Notification): Soft Rose (#FCE7F3) with Pink border (#DB2777)
  • Background: Ice Gray (#F1F5F9)

docs.title: Swimlanes
docs.text:
• Lane 1: System Access (Subscription, Auth)
  • Lane 2: Core Directory (School, Student, Teacher, Guardian)
  • Lane 3: Transactional Modules (Attendance, Homework, Exams, Fees, Payments)
  • Lane 4: Insights & Event Delivery (Reports, Dashboard, Notifications)

docs.title: draw.io Design Specification — Monitoring Flow
docs.text:
• File name: monitoring-flow.drawio
  • Source context: docs/04-Software-Requirements/software-requirements.md

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Detail the telemetry architecture and data flow of the EduSync platform. This diagram shows how log, metric, and trace telemetry is emitted from applications, databases, caches, queues, and external integrations, aggregated by an observability platform, and translated into dashboards and alerting triggers.

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• Telemetry Sources
  • Application (Spring Boot SaaS service)
  • PostgreSQL (Database)
  • Redis (Cache)
  • RabbitMQ (Queue)
  • External Providers (SMS, payment gateways, etc.)
  • Telemetry Buffers / Types
  • Logs (Standard output/error streams)
  • Metrics (Time-series performance metrics)
  • Traces (Distributed transaction traces)
  • Ingestion & Processing
  • Observability Platform (Elasticsearch/Logstash/Kibana, Prometheus/Grafana, or OpenTelemetry collector)
  • Output & Consumption
  • Operational Dashboards
  • Alerts & Escalations

docs.title: Layout
docs.text:
• Use a left-to-right flow with 4 distinct horizontal layers:
  • Sources (Far Left): Staged vertically. Place Application at the top center, and database/infrastructure blocks stacked below it.
  • Data Types (Middle-Left): Three circular or cylindrical shapes labeled Logs, Metrics, and Traces to show the pipeline divisions.
  • Processor (Middle-Right): A large central container labeled Observability Platform with inner components for ingestion, aggregation, and querying.
  • Consumers (Far Right): Two outputs (Operational Dashboards and Alerts & Escalations) stacked vertically.
  • Keep the connections straight and horizontal where possible to show data flow.

docs.title: Connections
docs.text:
• Connect Application directly to Logs, Metrics, and Traces.
  • Connect PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, and External Providers directly to Metrics (or to custom metric collectors).
  • Connect Logs, Metrics, and Traces into the Observability Platform container.
  • Connect Observability Platform to both Operational Dashboards and Alerts & Escalations on the right.
  • Use arrowheads representing the direction of data push/pull (e.g. Prometheus pulling metrics vs. application pushing logs/traces).

docs.title: Color Recommendations
docs.text:
• Telemetry Sources: Light Blue (#EFF6FF) with Indigo border (#3B82F6)
  • Logs: Pale Yellow (#FEF3C7) with Gold border (#D97706)
  • Metrics: Pale Purple (#F3E8FF) with Violet border (#8B5CF6)
  • Traces: Pale Rose (#FCE7F3) with Pink border (#DB2777)
  • Observability Platform: Light Teal (#E0F2FE) with Dark Teal border (#0D9488)
  • Outputs / Consumers: Pale Green (#ECFDF5) with Emerald border (#10B981)
  • Background: Soft Gray (#F8FAFC)

docs.title: Swimlanes
docs.text:
• Lane 1: Source Layer (Platform components generating telemetry)
  • Lane 2: Telemetry Pipeline (Data formatting: logs, metrics, traces)
  • Lane 3: Analytics Engine (Observability central processor)
  • Lane 4: Action & Visualization Layer (Dashboards, paging alerts)

docs.title: draw.io Design Specification — Product Architecture Context
docs.text:
• File name: product-architecture-context.drawio
  • Source context: docs/03-Product-Requirements/product-requirements.md

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Provide a professional architecture overview of the EduSync product context, showing how users, client applications, the API layer, domain modules, and platform services interact.

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• Client layer
  • Users
  • React web app
  • React Native mobile app
  • Application layer
  • Spring Boot REST API
  • Authentication and authorization
  • Domain modules
    • School
    • Student
    • Teacher
    • Guardian
    • Attendance
    • Homework
    • Examination
    • Fees
    • Payments
    • Reports
    • Dashboard
    • Notification
    • Subscription
  • Platform services
  • PostgreSQL
  • Redis
  • RabbitMQ
  • AWS S3
  • SMS, WhatsApp, Email, payment providers

docs.title: Layout
docs.text:
• Use a three-column canvas:
  • Client layer on the left
  • API and domain services in the center
  • Data, messaging, storage, and external providers on the right
  • Group domain modules inside a single container labeled Domain Modules.
  • Keep the API at the center of the diagram with clear directional lines to each platform capability.

docs.title: Connections
docs.text:
• Users -> web app and mobile app.
  • Web app and mobile app -> REST API.
  • REST API -> authentication and authorization.
  • REST API -> domain modules.
  • Domain modules -> PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, AWS S3.
  • RabbitMQ -> external communication and payment providers.
  • Keep relationships explicit and avoid overly dense cross-links.

docs.title: Color Recommendations
docs.text:
• Users and clients: light blue (#BFDBFE)
  • API and application services: blue (#2563EB)
  • Domain modules: orange (#F59E0B)
  • Data layer: purple (#7C3AED)
  • External integrations: amber (#D97706)
  • Background: white or very light gray (#F8FAFC)

docs.title: Swimlanes
docs.text:
• Recommended swimlanes: Client Layer, Application Layer, Platform & Integration Layer
  • Use container backgrounds to visually separate each lane.

docs.title: draw.io Design Specification — Proposed Future Workflow
docs.text:
• File name: proposed-future-workflow.drawio
  • Source context: docs/02-Business-Requirements/business-requirements.md

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Map the end-to-end operational flow of the proposed future EduSync platform. It demonstrates how tenant setup and master data feed into academic and financial management, leading to real-time dashboards and leadership-driven targeted communication, while feeding audit logs in parallel.

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• Setup & Master Data
  • School Tenant Setup
  • Master Data Configuration
  • Student & Guardian Records
  • Teacher & Employee Records
  • Daily Operations
  • Attendance Management
  • Fee Management
  • Examination Management
  • Homework & Assignments
  • Payments & Reconciliation
  • Insights & Leadership
  • Dashboards & Reports
  • Principal & Owner Decisions
  • Engagement & Communication
  • Targeted Communication
  • Parents & Students
  • Teachers & Employees
  • Compliance & Security
  • Audit Logs

docs.title: Layout
docs.text:
• Use a landscape layout with a top-to-bottom and left-to-right flow.
  • Organize the components across distinct horizontal swimlanes to maintain clarity of data lineage.
  • Position the core operational blocks (Attendance, Fees, Exams, Tasks) centrally to show parallel transaction streams.
  • The Audit Logs component should be placed in a separate lane at the bottom, receiving feeds from the various operations and communication components.

docs.title: Connections
docs.text:
• Connect School Tenant Setup to Master Data Configuration.
  • Connect Master Data Configuration to both Student & Guardian Records and Teacher & Employee Records.
  • Connect Student & Guardian Records to all four core operational modules: Attendance Management, Fee Management, Examination Management, and Homework & Assignments.
  • Connect Teacher & Employee Records to Attendance Management, Examination Management, and Homework & Assignments.
  • Connect Fee Management to Payments & Reconciliation.
  • Connect Payments & Reconciliation, Attendance Management, Examination Management, and Homework & Assignments directly to Dashboards & Reports.
  • Connect Dashboards & Reports to Principal & Owner Decisions.
  • Connect Principal & Owner Decisions to Targeted Communication.
  • Connect Targeted Communication to the receivers: Parents & Students and Teachers & Employees.
  • Draw dashed lines from Fee Management, Examination Management, Attendance Management, and Targeted Communication to Audit Logs to represent asynchronous audit writing.

docs.title: Color Recommendations
docs.text:
• Setup Layer: Pale Orange (#FFEDD5) with Amber border (#D97706)
  • Core Operations: Pale Blue (#EFF6FF) with Blue border (#2563EB)
  • Payments: Pale Green (#ECFDF5) with Emerald border (#10B981)
  • Dashboards & Decisions: Pale Indigo (#EEF2F6) with Indigo border (#4F46E5)
  • Communication & Stakeholders: Light Purple (#F5F3FF) with Purple border (#7C3AED)
  • Audit Logs: Light Gray (#F3F4F6) with Dark Gray border (#4B5563)
  • Background: Soft Gray (#F8FAFC)

docs.title: Swimlanes
docs.text:
• Lane 1: Setup & Identity (Tenant setup, Master Data, Student/Staff records)
  • Lane 2: Operational Workflows (Attendance, Fees, Exams, Tasks, Payments)
  • Lane 3: Analytics & Leadership (Dashboards, Decisions)
  • Lane 4: Audience Engagement (Communication, Parents, Staff)
  • Lane 5: Governance & Security (Audit Logs)
  • Separating these areas visually prevents cross-line clutter.

docs.title: draw.io Design Specification — Software System Context
docs.text:
• File name: software-system-context.drawio
  • Source context: docs/04-Software-Requirements/software-requirements.md

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Capture the full software system context for EduSync, including clients, edge services, application APIs, core platform services, messaging, storage, external integrations, and observability.

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• Client entry points
  • Web browser
  • Mobile device
  • Edge and experience layer
  • React web application
  • React Native Expo application
  • Nginx
  • Core application layer
  • Spring Boot REST API
  • Spring Security
  • Domain modules
  • Data and platform services
  • PostgreSQL
  • Redis
  • RabbitMQ
  • AWS S3
  • Integration and operations
  • Notification workers
  • SMS provider
  • WhatsApp provider
  • Email provider
  • Payment gateway
  • Centralized logs
  • Metrics
  • Distributed traces
  • Alerts

docs.title: Layout
docs.text:
• Use a left-to-right, layered layout.
  • Place users and clients on the far left.
  • Place edge and web/mobile application nodes in the middle-left.
  • Place the API and domain modules in the center.
  • Place databases, queues, storage, and external integrations on the right.
  • Place observability and alerting at the far right as a support layer.

docs.title: Connections
docs.text:
• Browser and mobile -> web/mobile apps -> Nginx -> REST API.
  • REST API -> Spring Security and domain modules.
  • REST API -> PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, AWS S3.
  • RabbitMQ -> notification workers -> SMS, WhatsApp, Email providers.
  • REST API -> payment gateway.
  • REST API -> logs, metrics, traces.
  • Metrics -> alerts.

docs.title: Color Recommendations
docs.text:
• Client layer: light blue (#DBEAFE)
  • Edge and app layer: blue (#2563EB)
  • Core platform layer: teal (#0F766E)
  • Data and messaging: purple (#7C3AED)
  • External providers: orange (#F59E0B)
  • Observability: green (#16A34A)
  • Background: white or pale gray (#F9FAFB)

docs.title: Swimlanes
docs.text:
• Recommended swimlanes: Client, Edge & Experience, Core Platform, Data & Integrations, Operations
  • The operations swimlane should be visually separated from the business transaction flow.

docs.title: draw.io Design Specification — SWOT Analysis
docs.text:
• File name: swot-analysis.drawio
  • Source context: docs/01-Vision/vision.md

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Present the strategic SWOT analysis for the EduSync platform in a professional 2x2 matrix grid. This format allows executive stakeholders to quickly compare internal factors (Strengths/Weaknesses) against external factors (Opportunities/Threats).

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• Core Pivot
  • EduSync SWOT (Central label)
  • Quadrant 1: Strengths (Internal, Positive)
  • Modern SaaS Architecture
  • AI-Powered Direction
  • Unified Platform Scope
  • Security-First Multi-Tenancy
  • Quadrant 2: Weaknesses (Internal, Negative)
  • New Brand
  • Implementation Complexity
  • Large Module Scope
  • Quadrant 3: Opportunities (External, Positive)
  • Digital Payments
  • Parent Mobile Expectations
  • School Group Expansion
  • AI Productivity
  • Quadrant 4: Threats (External, Negative)
  • Established ERP Vendors
  • Migration Resistance
  • Security and Privacy Risk
  • Integration Policy Changes

docs.title: Layout
docs.text:
• Use a landscape canvas with a 2x2 grid layout (four large quadrant boxes).
  • Place Strengths in the top-left and Weaknesses in the top-right.
  • Place Opportunities in the bottom-left and Threats in the bottom-right.
  • (Optional) Place a small circular or diamond-shaped badge containing SWOT at the center intersection of the grid lines to anchor the diagram.
  • Each quadrant should have a clear, bold header with an icon indicator or capital letter (S, W, O, T) and bulleted/stacked rectangular list cards representing the individual items.

docs.title: Connections
docs.text:
• Since this is a static matrix model rather than a process flow, there are no connecting arrows between the quadrant items.
  • Use clean, minimal visual borders to separate the four quadrants.

docs.title: Color Recommendations
docs.text:
• Strengths (S): Pale Green (#F0FDF4) with Emerald Green headers (#16A34A)
  • Weaknesses (W): Pale Yellow/Amber (#FFFBEB) with Amber/Orange headers (#D97706)
  • Opportunities (O): Pale Blue (#EFF6FF) with Royal Blue headers (#2563EB)
  • Threats (T): Pale Red (#FEF2F2) with Crimson/Red headers (#DC2626)
  • Central Badge: Neutral Slate Gray (#475569) with white text
  • Background: Soft Gray/Off-White (#F8FAFC)

docs.title: Swimlanes
docs.text:
• Swimlanes are represented by the 2x2 grid lines:
  • Horizontal Divider: Separates Internal factors (top) from External factors (bottom).
  • Vertical Divider: Separates Positive factors (left) from Negative factors (right).
  • Label these divider borders as "Internal Factors" (Top), "External Factors" (Bottom), "Helpful / Positive" (Left), and "Harmful / Negative" (Right).

docs.title: draw.io Design Specification — Strategic Vision Model
docs.text:
• File name: vision-model.drawio
  • Source context: docs/01-Vision/vision.md

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Present a structured, multi-tier capability map of the EduSync product vision. This diagram helps align stakeholders by grouping the platform's core capabilities (Academics, Security, AI, Leadership, Engagement) into high-level strategic domains stemming from a single vision anchor.

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• Vision Anchor
  • EduSync Vision
  • Strategic Domains
  • Unified School Operations
  • Secure Multi-Tenant SaaS
  • AI-Assisted Productivity
  • Data-Driven Leadership
  • Parent & Student Engagement
  • Platform Capabilities
  • School Operations Branch: Admissions, Attendance, Academics, Fees & Payments, Staff Workflows
  • SaaS Security Branch: Tenant Isolation, Role-Based Access, Audit Logs, Secure APIs
  • AI Productivity Branch: Drafting Assistance, Operational Insights, Risk Signals, Workflow Recommendations
  • Leadership Branch: Dashboards, Reports, Forecasting, Compliance Visibility
  • Engagement Branch: Mobile Access, Notifications, Fee Transparency, Academic Updates

docs.title: Layout
docs.text:
• Use a central hub-and-spoke or structured horizontal cascade layout (from left to right or top to bottom).
  • Cascade Layout (Recommended):
  • Top level: Single centered box representing EduSync Vision.
  • Second level: 5 horizontally spaced cards representing the Strategic Domains.
  • Third level: Vertical stacks of rectangular cards directly under each respective domain.
  • Group each branch inside a shaded background container matching the domain color scheme.
  • Use card style nodes with bold headers for the domains, and simple flat rectangles for the platform capabilities.

docs.title: Connections
docs.text:
• Connect EduSync Vision to all 5 Strategic Domains with thick, centered lines.
  • Connect each Strategic Domain to its child capability nodes. Use clean orthogonal connector paths (right angles) instead of direct diagonal lines to maintain a grid-like alignment.
  • No cross-domain links are allowed in this structure to keep it strictly hierarchical.

docs.title: Color Recommendations
docs.text:
• Vision Anchor: Deep Purple (#581C87) with white text
  • Unified School Operations Branch: Light Slate Blue (#EEF2F6) with Royal Blue headers (#2563EB)
  • SaaS Security Branch: Light Teal (#F0FDFA) with Dark Teal headers (#0D9488)
  • AI Productivity Branch: Light Rose (#FFF1F2) with Crimson headers (#E11D48)
  • Leadership Branch: Light Emerald (#ECFDF5) with Forest Green headers (#059669)
  • Engagement Branch: Light Amber (#FFFBEB) with Gold headers (#D97706)
  • Background: Soft Clean Gray (#F8FAFC)

docs.title: Swimlanes
docs.text:
• Swimlanes are not strictly required since it is a pure hierarchical card tree.
  • However, vertical columns or background containers for each of the 5 branches must be used to visually segregate the domains.

docs.title: draw.io Design Specification — Vision Opportunity Map
docs.text:
• File name: vision-opportunity-map.drawio
  • Source context: docs/01-Vision/vision.md

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Create a presentation-ready strategic map showing how market forces create school pain, which EduSync capabilities address that pain, and which business outcomes result.

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• Market forces
  • Mobile parent expectations
  • Digital payments adoption
  • Board and compliance complexity
  • AI adoption
  • School chain growth
  • School pain points
  • Communication fragmentation
  • Manual fee collection
  • Reporting burden
  • Administrative overload
  • Branch visibility gap
  • EduSync capabilities
  • Unified notifications
  • Integrated billing and payment
  • Structured reports
  • AI-assisted workflows
  • Multi-branch dashboards
  • Business outcomes
  • Higher parent trust
  • Improved cash flow
  • Management readiness
  • Productivity gains
  • Scalable governance

docs.title: Layout
docs.text:
• Use a wide landscape page.
  • Organize the canvas into four horizontal bands:
  • Market forces
  • School pain
  • EduSync capability
  • Business outcome
  • Place each band in a subtle container to preserve alignment.
  • Keep arrow paths short and evenly spaced to avoid visual clutter.

docs.title: Connections
docs.text:
• Connect each market force to one related pain point.
  • Connect each pain point to one capability.
  • Connect each capability to one outcome.
  • Use directional arrows with consistent spacing and avoid crossing lines where possible.

docs.title: Color Recommendations
docs.text:
• Market forces: teal (#2E86AB)
  • School pain: amber (#D97706)
  • EduSync capability: blue (#2563EB)
  • Business outcome: green (#15803D)
  • Background: light gray (#F8FAFC)

docs.title: Swimlanes
docs.text:
• Recommended swimlanes: Market, Pain, Capability, Outcome
  • Use thin divider lines between lanes to keep the diagram readable.

docs.title: Wireframes and UX Diagrams
docs.text:
This folder contains wireframes, user flow diagrams, and experience-oriented design artifacts for EduSync.

Use this location for:

  • user journey diagrams
  • wireframe references
  • interaction flow diagrams
  • UX and interface planning visuals
  • experience maps

These assets should support product design, UX planning, and frontend implementation discussions.
